one_line: Deep navy + cream + sage accent. The fintech aesthetic that killed the bright-blue-bank stereotype. Feels like Mercury, Brex, Ramp.

background.role: Warm cream/parchment, not pure white. Distinct from editorial-premium's slightly cooler #FAF8F4.

surface_navy.role: Deep navy used for hero sections, footers, accent panels. Signature.

text_primary.role: Deep navy as text on cream — the signature combination.

text_on_navy.role: Cream text when the bg is navy.

accent_sage.role: Sage green — primary accent. Used for CTAs, success states, financial-positive indicators.

accent_gold.role: Olive-gold — secondary accent. Used sparingly for premium signaling.

success.role: Same as sage — incoming money, positive states.

danger.role: Muted terracotta-red. Bank-style outgoing money, errors. NOT vivid #FF0000.

rules: Cream + navy + sage is the signature triad. Avoid bright blues (#0066CC, #2563EB) — those are 1990s-bank blue, the trope Mercury rejected
Sage is the PRIMARY accent. Gold is reserved for premium tier indicators / occasional flourishes
Money color rules: incoming = sage. Outgoing = neutral text (don't paint it red unless it's an error). Errors = muted terracotta
Never use the green/red flashing-light treatment some fintech uses for transaction logs. Numbers stay text_primary; the sign + or - carries direction

display.family: Söhne, Tiempos Headline, Playfair Display, Georgia, serif
display.rule: Mercury uses Söhne. Brex uses serifs in marketing. Pick serif for marketing surfaces (premium); pick sans for product UI (legibility).

use_for: Use INSTEAD of display serif when building product UI (dashboards, app screens)

mono.family: Söhne Mono, JetBrains Mono, ui-monospace, monospace

rules: Marketing: serif display for headlines, sans for body (editorial-feel)
Product UI: sans throughout (legibility for financial data)
Tabular numbers MANDATORY on any money column or financial table
Base 15px (between editorial-premium's 16 and linear-product's 14) — confident readability
Default radius: md (8px). Slightly more rounded than vercel-dev / linear-product to signal warmth without going playful
Never pills on rectangular buttons. Account-balance cards may use lg radius for tactile feel
Cards in marketing: subtle 1px border in cream-tone OR soft shadow (not both)
Cards in product: 1px border only (data clarity beats softness)

money_display.size: varies by context — large for hero, base for tables
money_display.rule: Money goes in mono. Positive amounts may use accent_sage for the integer dollar portion ONLY if the design emphasises positive flow (e.g. a 'received' inbox). Default is text_primary.

account_card.rule: Account name in body weight 500. Balance below in mono, larger size, text_primary. NEVER huge green-glowing money.

marketing_hero.structure: Left: serif headline + restrained subhead + ONE sage CTA. Right: real product UI screenshot. No floating coins, no abstract finance imagery.
do: Single confident statement, not a list of features
Subtle navy section below hero with stat cards in cream-on-navy
dont: Stock photos of 'business people shaking hands'
Floating cards with mock transaction data tilted at fancy angles

product_dashboard.structure: Sidebar with account list (each with mono balance). Main: transaction table with date | description | amount columns, amount right-aligned in mono.
do: Tiny status badges for transaction state (cleared, pending)
Clean separators between days in tx list
dont: Charts as the main thing — let the table dominate

animations.philosophy: Tasteful, deliberate. Money movements need to feel solid, not flashy.

patterns.balance_update: Number tween from old to new value, 400ms ease-out. NO celebratory color flash.
patterns.transaction_arrival: New row fades in from top, 300ms. The list scrolls smoothly to accommodate.

anti_patterns: confetti on milestones — cheapens the premium feel
